title: fix: remove tier-upgrade logic from app approval flow
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
## Summary
- Tiers are frozen; pollen now comes from Quests (frontend already shows "Tiers are going away").
- The app-approval bot still upgraded users to Flower tier and announced "0.4 pollen per hour" — that mechanism no longer applies.
- Both bot messages now point contributors to the existing **Contribute a pull request** quest (5 Quest Pollen, claimed from their enter.pollinations.ai dashboard) instead of writing tier state to D1.

## Changes
- `app-upgrade-tier.yml` → renamed `app-merge-celebration.yml`: dropped the D1 tier update/verify steps (`tier-update-user.ts update-tier`/`verify-tier`), now just posts a celebration comment linking to the quest claim.
- `app-check-registration.yml`: dropped the tier-upgrade step, kept the read-only registration check, points newly-registered users to the quest claim instead.

## Test plan
- [x] Validated both workflow YAML files parse correctly
- [ ] Merge and confirm the next app-submission PR merge posts the new copy correctly

gist.headline: App approvals meet Quests
gist.blurb: We've updated the app submission bot to point contributors straight to the new Quest system. Merging an app now earns you a flat 5 Quest Pollen instead of the old tier upgrades!
gist.summary: The app-approval GitHub workflows have been updated to remove legacy Flower tier upgrades, instead directing contributors to claim 5 Quest Pollen via the 'Contribute a pull request' quest.
gist.impact: Contributors submitting new apps will now receive a flat 5 Quest Pollen reward upon merge rather than a recurring hourly drip.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee and the Monitor Robot celebrating a merged pull request. The robot is holding a glowing '5' or a quest scroll, while the bee is wearing a little adventurer's hat.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny adventurer's hat, high-fiving a chunky monitor-head robot. The robot's screen displays a glowing '5' and a golden quest scroll. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and warm peach gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
